_doc: Phase 5 Plan 05-06 — filled-slots SHADOW-log canary. Inspector fills measured_zs_ohm on circuit 3 in turn 1 (pre-ask state via the test harness's pre_seeded_circuits). On turn 2 the model emits ask_user(reason='out_of_range_circuit', context_field='measured_zs_ohm', context_circuit=3) — i.e. it would be asking about a slot that's already filled. Phase 5's filled-slots-shadow adapter MUST emit ONE 'stage6.filled_slots_would_suppress' log row but the ask MUST STILL FIRE (Plan 05-02: shadow ≠ suppress). The wrapper proceeds normally; the inner dispatcher returns answered:true; the budget increments. Per Plan 05-06 design: gate_fixture:true — 1 successful ask is benign for aggregate metrics.
_requirement: STB-03 — filled-slots filter runs in shadow mode on the tool-call path: logs when it would have suppressed but does NOT suppress. Pitfall 6 carry-over: shadow logger emits would-suppress regardless of whether the legacy filter would actually have done so.
transcript_summary: Turn 1 fills Zs on circuit 3 (state pre-seeded). Turn 2 the model emits a refill-style ask_user for the same (field, circuit). Shadow log fires; ask still proceeds.

input.question: Was that Zs reading for circuit three?
